keywords.description: interface - Defines a contract for object structure. Used for type checking and ensuring objects have required properties.
keywords.example: interface User { name: string; age: number; }

keywords.description: type - Creates type aliases for complex types. More flexible than interfaces, can represent unions, intersections, and primitives.
enum - Defines a set of named constants. Useful for representing a fixed set of values.
namespace - Organizes code into logical groups. Prevents global scope pollution and name conflicts.
module - Declares a module (legacy syntax, prefer ES6 modules). Used for ambient module declarations.
keywords.example: declare module 'my-module' { export function fn(): void; }

keywords.description: declare - Tells TypeScript about code that exists elsewhere. Used for ambient declarations and .d.ts files.
public - Makes class members accessible from anywhere. Default visibility for class members.
private - Restricts class member access to within the class only. Enforces encapsulation.
protected - Allows access within class and subclasses only. Used in inheritance scenarios.
readonly - Marks properties that can only be set during initialization. Prevents modification after creation.
abstract - Defines abstract classes and methods that must be implemented by subclasses.
keywords.example: abstract class Animal { abstract makeSound(): void; }

keywords.description: implements - Ensures a class satisfies an interface contract. Used for interface implementation.
extends - Creates inheritance between classes or interfaces. Enables code reuse and polymorphism.
as - Type assertion that tells TypeScript to treat a value as a specific type.
is - Type predicate used in type guard functions to narrow types.
keywords.example: function isString(val: any): val is string { return typeof val === 'string'; }

keywords.description: keyof - Creates a union type of all property keys of an object type.
typeof - Gets the type of a value or variable. Used for type queries.
keywords.example: const config = { api: 'url' }; type Config = typeof config;

keywords.description: infer - Infers types within conditional type expressions. Advanced type manipulation tool.
keywords.example: type ReturnType<T> = T extends (...args: any[]) => infer R ? R : any;

keywords.description: never - Represents values that never occur. Used for exhaustive checks and impossible states.
keywords.example: function throwError(msg: string): never { throw new Error(msg); }

keywords.description: unknown - Type-safe alternative to any. Requires type checking before use.
keywords.example: let value: unknown; if (typeof value === 'string') { console.log(value.length); }

keywords.description: any - Disables type checking for a value. Use sparingly as it defeats TypeScript's purpose.
void - Represents the absence of a return value. Used for functions that don't return anything.
string - Primitive type for text data. Includes string literals and template strings.
number - Primitive type for numeric values. Includes integers and floating-point numbers.
boolean - Primitive type for true/false values.
null - Represents intentional absence of value. One of TypeScript's primitive types.
undefined - Represents uninitialized or missing values.
symbol - Unique and immutable primitive value. Often used as object property keys.
bigint - Primitive type for arbitrarily large integers.
object - Represents non-primitive types. Any value that isn't primitive.
Array - Generic type for arrays. Can specify element type.
Promise - Generic type for asynchronous operations.
Record - Utility type for creating object types with specific keys and value types.
Partial - Makes all properties of a type optional. Useful for updates and patches.
Required - Makes all properties of a type required. Opposite of Partial.
Readonly - Makes all properties of a type readonly. Prevents modification.
Pick - Creates a type by picking specific properties from another type.
Omit - Creates a type by omitting specific properties from another type.
Exclude - Excludes types from a union. Filters out specific types.
Extract - Extracts types from a union that match a pattern.
NonNullable - Removes null and undefined from a type.
ReturnType - Extracts the return type of a function.
Parameters - Extracts parameter types of a function as a tuple.
InstanceType - Gets the instance type of a constructor function.
Awaited - Recursively unwraps Promise types to get the resolved value type.
const - Declares a constant variable with type inference. Can use 'as const' for literal types.
let - Declares a mutable variable with optional type annotation.
var - Function-scoped variable declaration. Avoid in modern TypeScript, use let/const.
function - Declares a function with typed parameters and return type.
keywords.example: function add(a: number, b: number): number { return a + b; }

keywords.description: async - Declares an asynchronous function that returns a Promise.
await - Waits for a Promise to resolve. Can only be used in async functions.
class - Declares a class with typed properties and methods.
constructor - Special method for initializing class instances with typed parameters.
keywords.example: constructor(private name: string, public age: number) {}

keywords.description: static - Defines class-level members accessible without instantiation.
keywords.example: static create(): User { return new User(); }

keywords.description: get - Defines a getter accessor with return type annotation.
keywords.example: get fullName(): string { return this.firstName + ' ' + this.lastName; }

keywords.description: set - Defines a setter accessor with typed parameter.
import - Imports modules with type information preserved.
export - Exports values, types, interfaces, and type aliases.
default - Marks the default export of a module.
this - Refers to the current instance. Can be typed with 'this: Type' parameter.
super - Calls parent class constructor or methods in inheritance.
new - Creates instances of classes with type checking.
satisfies - Validates that a value matches a type without widening it. TypeScript 4.9+.
asserts - Used in assertion function signatures to narrow types.
keywords.example: function assert(condition: any): asserts condition { if (!condition) throw new Error(); }

keywords.description: override - Explicitly marks a method as overriding a parent class method. Enables compile-time checks.
accessor - Auto-accessor keyword (TS 4.9+). Creates private backing field with auto getter/setter.
using - Explicit resource management (TS 5.2+). Auto-disposes when leaving scope via Symbol.dispose.
keywords.example: using file = openFile('data.txt'); // auto-disposed at end of scope

keywords.description: await using - Async resource management (TS 5.2+). Auto-disposes via Symbol.asyncDispose.
keywords.example: await using conn = await getConnection(); // auto-disposed at end of scope

keywords.description: import type - Import only type information, erased at runtime. Prevents accidental side effects.
export type - Export only type information. Explicit type-only export.
in - Used in mapped types to iterate over keys, or as type narrowing operator.
keywords.example: type Flags = { [K in keyof T]: boolean }; if ('name' in obj) { }

keywords.description: out - Covariant type parameter variance annotation (TS 4.7+). Type can only appear in output positions.
in - Contravariant type parameter variance annotation (TS 4.7+). Type can only appear in input positions.
keywords.example: interface Consumer<in T> { accept(value: T): void; }

keywords.description: instanceof - Runtime type check that narrows types. Works with class instances.
ConstructorParameters - Extracts parameter types of a constructor function as a tuple.
ThisParameterType - Extracts the type of the 'this' parameter from a function type.
OmitThisParameter - Removes the 'this' parameter from a function type.
ThisType - Sets the type of 'this' in an object literal. Used with --noImplicitThis.
keywords.example: type ObjectDescriptor<D, M> = { data?: D; methods?: M & ThisType<D & M>; };

keywords.description: Uppercase - Intrinsic string manipulation type. Converts string literal type to uppercase.
Lowercase - Intrinsic string manipulation type. Converts string literal type to lowercase.
Capitalize - Intrinsic string manipulation type. Capitalizes first character.
Uncapitalize - Intrinsic string manipulation type. Lowercases first character.
NoInfer - Prevents type inference from a specific position (TS 5.4+).
keywords.example: function fn<T>(value: T, defaultVal: NoInfer<T>): T { return value ?? defaultVal; }

keywords.description: Map - Generic key-value collection with any type as key. Maintains insertion order.
keywords.example: const map = new Map<string, User>(); map.set('alice', user);

keywords.description: Set - Generic collection of unique values.
WeakMap - Map with weak references to keys (must be objects). Allows garbage collection.
WeakSet - Set with weak references to values (must be objects). Allows garbage collection.
ReadonlyArray - Array type that prevents mutations. No push, pop, splice, etc.
ReadonlyMap - Map type that prevents mutations. No set, delete, clear.
keywords.example: const config: ReadonlyMap<string, number> = new Map([['port', 3000]]);

keywords.description: ReadonlySet - Set type that prevents mutations. No add, delete, clear.
Iterable - Type for objects implementing the iteration protocol (Symbol.iterator).
keywords.example: function process<T>(items: Iterable<T>): void { for (const item of items) { } }

keywords.description: AsyncIterable - Type for async iteration protocol (Symbol.asyncIterator).
keywords.example: async function* generate(): AsyncIterable<number> { yield 1; yield 2; }

keywords.description: Iterator - Type with next() method returning { value, done }.
Generator - Type for generator functions. Generator<Yield, Return, Next>.
keywords.example: function* range(start: number, end: number): Generator<number> { /* ... */ }

keywords.description: AsyncGenerator - Type for async generator functions.
PromiseLike - Type for thenable objects. More general than Promise.
keywords.example: function wrap<T>(value: T | PromiseLike<T>): Promise<T> { return Promise.resolve(value); }

keywords.description: PropertyKey - Union type of valid property key types: string | number | symbol.
keywords.example: function getProperty(obj: object, key: PropertyKey): unknown { }

keywords.description: Disposable - Interface for explicit resource management (TS 5.2+). Implements Symbol.dispose.
AsyncDisposable - Async version of Disposable (TS 5.2+). Implements Symbol.asyncDispose.
keywords.example: class Connection implements AsyncDisposable { async [Symbol.asyncDispose]() { await this.close(); } }

keywords.description: const type parameter - Infer literal/readonly types from generic arguments (TS 5.0+).
keywords.example: function createTuple<const T extends readonly unknown[]>(...args: T): T { return args; }

snippets.description: Branded / nominal type for type-safe IDs

snippets.content:
type ${1:JSONValue} =
    | string
    | number
    | boolean
    | null
    | ${1:JSONValue}[]
    | { [key: string]: ${1:JSONValue} };

snippets.description: Result type for error handling (Either pattern)
Mapped type with template literal key remapping

snippets.content:
type ${1:UnpackPromise}<T> = T extends Promise<infer U> ? U : T;
type ${2:UnpackArray}<T> = T extends (infer U)[] ? U : T;

snippets.description: Convert enum values to union type
Interface with methods and optional properties
Const type parameter for literal inference (TS 5.0+)
Strict Omit that only allows existing keys
snippets.content: type StrictOmit<T, K extends keyof T> = Pick<T, Exclude<keyof T, K>>;

snippets.description: Type-safe dot-notation paths of an object
snippets.content:
type ${1:Paths}<T> = T extends object
    ? { [K in keyof T]: K extends string
        ? T[K] extends object
            ? K | `${K}.${${1:Paths}<T[K]>}`
            : K
        : never
    }[keyof T]
    : never;

snippets.description: Derive type from const object (as const pattern)
Class with getter/setter and private field
